reasoning: The base rate for a random month exceeding the 12-month average in a conflict setting is generally less than 50% due to right-skewed fatality distributions (where a few high-fatality months pull the average up). In this specific case, evidence strongly suggests a de-escalation in Yemen during the target period (Feb 20 - Mar 21, 2026). The Southern Transitional Council (STC) was dissolved in early February following a capitulation in January, effectively ending a major source of proxy violence. Furthermore, the Houthis have reportedly adopted a cautious stance, avoiding escalation in the broader regional Israel-Iran conflict and engaging in negotiations with Saudi Arabia. Given the 10% decrease in clashes earlier in the year and the recent political stabilizing events, fatalities are highly likely to be below the 360-day average of 212.25.
